Open Saturdays by Appointment contactus@pleasantendodontics. com (469) 298-3161 Pleasant Endodontics Home About us Treatment Contact More Menu Endodontic (Root Canal) Treatment What is Root Canal Therapy. Endodontic therapy, commonly known as a root canal, is performed when the pulp of the tooth becomes diseased as the result of exposure to bacteria from dental caries, trauma or other causes.

Modareszadeh has remained active in his field, using his many years of specialized training and experience to help patients as well as teach and research. He lives with his wife and twin boys in Plano, Texas. ©2022 Pleasant Endodontics Powered by Webnode

com (469) 298-3161 Pleasant Endodontics Home About us Treatment Contact More Menu Pleasant Endodontics Hunter's Glen Office Complex 5509 Pleasant Valley Dr Suite #20 Plano, TX 75023 E: contactus@pleasantendodontics.

The involved tooth may be symptomatic, and you may experience pain, or the tooth could be totally symptom free. In either case, in order to save the tooth, the diseased pulp should be removed and the root canals should be disinfected and filled. Advances in this common dental procedure have improved the success at preserving and maintaining your natural teeth.

Make an Appointment Appointments (469) 298-3161 Address 5509 Pleasant Valley Dr, Suite #20 Plano, TX 75023 (Hunter's Glen Office Complex) Dr. Mahmoud R. Modareszadeh, DDS, MSD, MSD Meet Dr. Modareszadeh Schedule an appointment Endodontics Root Canal Therapy We specialize in providing advanced endodontic treatment. Consultations Schedule a consultation to determine whether root canal therapy is the right option for you.

Modareszadeh ("Dr. M") is an Endodontist, a dentist trained specially in Endodontic Therapy. He has over 20 years of experience treating patients and teaching Endodontics. Dr. Modareszadeh completed his Endodontic residency at the Case Western Reserve University School of Dental Medicine in Cleveland, Ohio. He has served as faculty at the University of Tennessee Health Science Center in Memphis and Azad University School of Dentistry in Tehran. Dr.

When a tooth experiences decay, root canal therapy is a safe and healthy alternative to tooth removal and dental implants, letting you keep your natural tooth. Our practice uses state-of-the-art technologies, such as dental operating microscopes and advanced imaging to ensure your natural smile. Contrary to misled beliefs about this treatment, root canal treatment is used to relieve pain. Read more about root canal therapy here. Our Specialist Mahmoud R. Modareszadeh, DDS, MSD, MSD Dr.

Watch the following video to learn more about Root Canal Treatment: ©2022 Pleasant Endodontics Powered by Webnode

Endodontic therapy, commonly known as a root canal, is performed when the pulp of the tooth becomes diseased as the result of exposure to bacteria from dental caries, trauma or other causes. The involved tooth may be symptomatic, and you may experience pain, or the tooth could be totally symptom free. In either case, in order to save the tooth, the diseased pulp should be removed and the root canals should be disinfected and filled.

Advances in this common dental procedure have improved the success at preserving and maintaining your natural teeth. Watch the following video to learn more about Root Canal Treatment: ©2022 Pleasant Endodontics Powered by Webnode
